Container.Name: cue_pipeline
Container.Overview: Watches your downloads for CUE + audio, splits/tags disc images, hands pre-split albums to Lidarr via ManualImport, force-imports clear matches, de-duplicates against the library, and cleans up sources. Runs alongside Lidarr in the same /downloads + /music namespace. LLM (Ollama) is optional.
Container.Description: cue_pipeline -- automatic CUE splitting + Lidarr import helper. Behavioral settings are editable below; anything not shown lives in /config/config.yaml.

Config.Name: Config / appdata
Config.Description: Holds config.yaml, logs, ledger and audit files. Must be writable by 99:100.

Config.Name: Downloads
Config.Description: Same host path Lidarr's /downloads points at -- where finished music downloads land (the watch root).

Config.Name: Music library
Config.Description: Same host path Lidarr's /music points at. The library sits at /music/Music.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone for log timestamps.

Config.Name: Lidarr URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your Lidarr instance.

Config.Name: Lidarr API key
Config.Description: Lidarr -> Settings -> General -> API Key.

Config.Name: LLM enabled (master switch)
Config.Description: MASTER SWITCH. false = no LLM at all and EVERY LLM_* field below is ignored (GPU stays free). true = use the provider+URL+model below. The LLM only auto-repairs malformed .cue files -- optional.

Config.Name: LLM provider
Config.Description: Only matters if enabled=true. Pick ONE and set base URL + model + key to MATCH it. 'ollama' = local GPU, no key. 'openai' = cloud (Gemini/OpenAI/Groq/OpenRouter), needs API key, no GPU.

Config.Name: LLM base URL (must match provider)
Config.Description: Only if enabled=true. MUST match provider: Gemini -> https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai ; OpenAI -> https://api.openai.com/v1 ; ollama -> http://HOST:11434 .

Config.Name: LLM model (must match provider)
Config.Description: Only if enabled=true. MUST match provider: Gemini -> gemini-2.0-flash ; OpenAI -> gpt-4o-mini ; ollama -> qwen2.5:14b .

Config.Name: LLM API key (cloud only)
Config.Description: Required for provider=openai (Gemini/OpenAI/...). Get a free Gemini key at aistudio.google.com. IGNORED for ollama and when enabled=false.

Config.Name: Delete source folder on success
Config.Description: DESTRUCTIVE. After a verified import, delete the whole source download folder. true/false.

Config.Name: Delete originals on success
Config.Description: After import, delete the source disc image + .cue (used when the folder isn't deleted wholesale). true/false.

Config.Name: Fill monitored gaps only (discography logic)
Config.Description: For messy discography grabs: only import a folder if it maps to an album Lidarr MONITORS and is INCOMPLETE. Skips already-complete albums (redundant) and skips comps/live/best-of with no monitored target (instead of thrashing on them), and keeps the edition whose track count is closest to Lidarr's release when duplicates exist. true/false.

Config.Name: Transcode DTS-CD to 5.1 FLAC
Config.Description: DTS audio CDs rip to raw .dts surround streams (no tags, no cue) that Lidarr can't ingest. When on, each .dts is transcoded to a channel-preserving (5.1) FLAC, tagged from the filename + folder name (LLM-assisted when there's no 'Artist - Album' separator), then imported normally. true/false.

Config.Name: Transcode lossless to FLAC
Config.Description: Re-encode pre-split lossless-but-not-FLAC tracks (.ape/.wv/.wav/.aiff/.tak/.tta/.shn and ALAC .m4a) to FLAC before import, preserving tags/bit-depth/sample-rate, so the library is uniformly FLAC. FLAC is left as-is; lossy (mp3/aac/ogg/opus/wma) and DSD are never touched. true/false.

Config.Name: Force-import on exact/near count match
Config.Description: Force-import when file count matches (or nearly matches) a Lidarr release, even if titles don't. true/false.

Config.Name: Force-import: max % missing
Config.Description: Allow up to this %% of an album's tracks to be missing and still force-import.

Config.Name: Force-import: max % extra
Config.Description: Above this %% of extra tracks it's treated as a compilation and left alone (prevents cramming).

Config.Name: Match floor %
Config.Description: Override Lidarr's 80%% match rule down to this floor for soft mismatches.

Config.Name: Library audit (scheduled, change-gated)
Config.Description: Periodically reconcile the on-disk library with Lidarr, only when it changed. true/false.

Config.Name: Queue reaper (clear stuck downloads)
Config.Description: Periodically clear FULLY-DOWNLOADED-but-stuck torrents from Lidarr's queue -- compilations, unmatched grabs and title-mismatches Lidarr will never import. Essential for mass discography imports where these otherwise pile up forever. true/false.

Config.Name: Queue reaper: interval (s)
Config.Description: How often to sweep the queue (seconds, min 60).

Config.Name: Queue reaper: grace (min)
Config.Description: A torrent must sit finished-and-stuck this many minutes before it's reaped, giving Lidarr and the pipeline time to import it first. Never touches a still-downloading torrent.

Config.Name: Queue reaper: delete from qBittorrent
Config.Description: DESTRUCTIVE. Also delete the torrent + its data from qBittorrent (required for the reap to stick -- otherwise Lidarr re-adds the row). WARNING: stops seeding -- a hit-and-run risk on PRIVATE trackers. true/false.

Config.Name: Queue reaper: blocklist on reap
Config.Description: Blocklist the release when reaping so Lidarr won't re-grab the exact same one. Off by default (we reap junk, not bad releases). true/false.

Config.Name: Purge-imported sweep
Config.Description: Periodically delete download folders whose album Lidarr ALREADY has fully -- the continuous form of the process-time pre-check that also reclaims re-downloads the pipeline skipped. Safe: only fully-in-library albums are removed; partial/unmatched/video/disc-subfolders are never touched and in-flight folders are skipped. true/false.

Config.Name: Purge-imported: dry run
Config.Description: DRY RUN by default -- only LOGS what it would delete. Watch a few passes in the log, then set false to actually reclaim the space. true/false.

Config.Name: Purge-imported: interval (s)
Config.Description: How often to run the purge sweep (seconds, min 300).

Config.Name: Purge-imported: min stable (s)
Config.Description: A folder must be untouched this many seconds before it can be purged, so an in-flight download/import is never removed mid-write.

Config.Name: qBittorrent URL
Config.Description: qBittorrent Web UI URL (for selective-download).

Config.Name: qBittorrent user
Config.Description: qBittorrent Web UI username.

Config.Name: qBittorrent password
Config.Description: qBittorrent Web UI password.

Config.Name: qBit auto-deselect
Config.Description: Auto-deselect (skip) albums already in the library on incomplete music torrents, so a discography grab only downloads what's missing. true/false.

Config.Name: qBit auto-deselect interval (s)
Config.Description: How often to poll qBittorrent when auto-deselect is on (min 10). Low = catches a new torrent almost as soon as it's added, before bandwidth leaks on albums you already have.

Config.Name: qBit pause-scan on add
Config.Description: Pause a freshly-seen torrent the instant it's noticed, deselect the albums you already own, then resume -- so a .torrent add never downloads the unwanted albums at all. Turn off to deselect without pausing (a little bandwidth may leak before deselect). true/false.

Config.Name: qBit category filter
Config.Description: Only act on torrents in this qBittorrent category (blank = all).

Config.Name: AcoustID fingerprint identify
Config.Description: At import time, if a download's TAGS can't identify it, fingerprint the audio (Chromaprint -> AcoustID -> MusicBrainz) to recover the real artist/album so it can still be imported. Best-effort, throttled ~3 req/s, never fatal. true/false.

Config.Name: AcoustID API key
Config.Description: Free AcoustID application key from https://acoustid.org/new-application (register an 'Installed application'). Short string like cSpUJKpD.
